tex:
Here is part of a bus timetable.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
Abbots & 06 50 & 08 25 & 09 20 \\
\hline
Callet & 07 12 & 08 47 & 09 42 \\
\hline
North Moor & 07 30 & 09 05 & 10 00 \\
\hline
South Moor & 07 37 & 09 12 & 10 07 \\
\hline
Centre Point & 08 00 & 09 35 & 10 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Rashid catches the 09 20 bus at Abbots.
\newline

Find the time the bus arrives at South Moor.
\newline

(b) Annisa leaves home at $8.27\,\text{am}$ and takes 25 minutes to walk to the bus stop at Callet.
\newline
\\
She catches the next bus to Centre Point.
\newline

Find the total time, in minutes, for her journey from leaving home to arriving at Centre Point.
\newline

(c) The distance from Abbots to Centre Point is $29.4\,\text{km}$.
\newline
\\
Each bus takes the same time for the journey.
\newline

Calculate the average speed of a bus for this journey.
\newline
\\
Give your answer in kilometres per hour.
\newline

(d) On one journey, all 56 seats on the bus are filled.
\newline
\\
The ratio of adults to children on this journey is   adults : children $= 5 : 3$.
\newline
\\
The cost for an adult ticket is \$2.80.
\newline
\\
The cost for a child ticket is $\dfrac{3}{4}$ of the adult cost.
\newline

Work out the total cost of the tickets for this journey.
\newline

_rawBody:
Here is part of a bus timetable.

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
Abbots & 06 50 & 08 25 & 09 20 \\
\hline
Callet & 07 12 & 08 47 & 09 42 \\
\hline
North Moor & 07 30 & 09 05 & 10 00 \\
\hline
South Moor & 07 37 & 09 12 & 10 07 \\
\hline
Centre Point & 08 00 & 09 35 & 10 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\begin{parts}
\item Rashid catches the 09 20 bus at Abbots.

Find the time the bus arrives at South Moor.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Annisa leaves home at 8.27\,am and takes 25 minutes to walk to the bus stop at Callet.\\
She catches the next bus to Centre Point.

Find the total time, in minutes, for her journey from leaving home to arriving at Centre Point.

\AnswerLine[][min]
\Marks{2}

\item The distance from Abbots to Centre Point is 29.4\,km.\\
Each bus takes the same time for the journey.

Calculate the average speed of a bus for this journey.\\
Give your answer in kilometres per hour.

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{2}

\item On one journey, all 56 seats on the bus are filled.\\
The ratio of adults to children on this journey is \quad adults : children $= 5 : 3$.\\
The cost for an adult ticket is \$2.80.\\
The cost for a child ticket is $\dfrac{3}{4}$ of the adult cost.

Work out the total cost of the tickets for this journey.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{4}

\end{parts}

tex:
(a) 

(i)  Draw the image of triangle $T$ after a reflection in the line $y = x$.
\newline

 Draw the image of triangle $T$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

 Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $T$ onto triangle $A$.
\newline

(b) A quadrilateral $P$ is enlarged by a scale factor of 1.2 to give quadrilateral $Q$.
\newline
\\
The area of quadrilateral $P$ is $20\,\text{cm}^2$.
\newline

Calculate the area of quadrilateral $Q$.
\newline

qtype: Percentage calculation from data, simplifying ratios
tex:
(a) The table shows the numbers of tigers reported to be living in the wild in the year 2014 in some countries.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Country & Number \\
\hline
India & 2226 \\
\hline
Indonesia & 371 \\
\hline
Nepal & 198 \\
\hline
Bangladesh & 106 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Using the table,

(b) find the number of tigers in Nepal as a percentage of the number of tigers in Bangladesh,

(c) find the ratio   tigers in Bangladesh : tigers in Indonesia : tigers in India,   giving your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

{...} : ]

 Five years later, the number of tigers reported in India was 2967.
\newline

Find the percentage increase in the population of tigers in India.
\newline

 The number of tigers in India in the year 2014 is approximately 30.48\% greater than in the year 2010.
\newline

Find the number of tigers in India in the year 2010.
\newline
\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest integer.
\newline

At the start of June, a hive has a population of 2000 bees.
\newline
\\
Three months after the start of June the hive has a population of 2662 bees.
\newline

The population of this hive can be calculated using the formula
\[P = ab^x,\]
where $P$ is the population of the hive $x$ months after the start of June.
\newline

By finding the value of $a$ and the value of $b$, calculate the population of the hive 7 months after the start of June.
\newline
\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest integer.
\newline

content.content:
The table shows the numbers of tigers reported to be living in the wild in the year 2014 in some countries.
\newline

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Country & Number \\
\hline
India & 2226 \\
\hline
Indonesia & 371 \\
\hline
Nepal & 198 \\
\hline
Bangladesh & 106 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
find the number of tigers in Nepal as a percentage of the number of tigers in Bangladesh,

find the ratio \quad tigers in Bangladesh : tigers in Indonesia : tigers in India, \quad giving your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

_postParts.content:
\subpart Five years later, the number of tigers reported in India was 2967.

Find the percentage increase in the population of tigers in India.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{2}

\subpart The number of tigers in India in the year 2014 is approximately 30.48\% greater than in the year 2010.

Find the number of tigers in India in the year 2010.\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest integer.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{3}

\end{subparts}

\item At the start of June, a hive has a population of 2000 bees.\\
Three months after the start of June the hive has a population of 2662 bees.

The population of this hive can be calculated using the formula
\[P = ab^x,\]
where $P$ is the population of the hive $x$ months after the start of June.

By finding the value of $a$ and the value of $b$, calculate the population of the hive 7 months after the start of June.\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest integer.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{5}

\end{parts}

tex:
A regular 12-sided polygon has side length $6\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(a) Show that one interior angle of the polygon is $150°$.
\newline

(b) The polygon is enclosed by a circle, centre $O$, so that each vertex touches the circumference of the circle.
\newline

(i)  Show that the radius, $AO$, of the circle is $11.6\,\text{cm}$, correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

 Calculate

(c) the circumference of the circle,

(d) the perimeter of the shaded **minor** segment formed by the chord $AB$.
\newline

The regular 12-sided polygon is the cross-section of a prism of length $2\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the prism.
\newline

content.content:
Show that one interior angle of the polygon is $150°$.
\newline

The polygon is enclosed by a circle, centre $O$, so that each vertex touches the circumference of the circle.
\newline
the circumference of the circle,

the perimeter of the shaded \textbf{minor} segment formed by the chord $AB$.
\newline

stem.content:
A regular 12-sided polygon has side length $6\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

tex:
The time, $t$ minutes, taken by each of 80 people to travel to work is recorded.
\newline
\\
The table shows information about these times.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & $0 < t \leqslant 5$ & $5 < t \leqslant 10$ & $10 < t \leqslant 20$ & $20 < t \leqslant 35$ & $35 < t \leqslant 60$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 3 & 7 & 18 & 28 & 24 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) (i)  Write down the class interval containing the median time.
\newline

{...}]

 Calculate an estimate of the mean time.
\newline

(b) (i)  One of these 80 people is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this person took longer than 10 minutes to travel to work.
\newline
\\
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

 Two people are chosen at random from those taking 20 minutes or less to travel to work.
\newline

Calculate the probability that one of these people took 5 minutes or less and the other took more than 5 minutes.
\newline

(c) (i)  Use the frequency table to complete the cumulative frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & $t \leqslant 5$ & $t \leqslant 10$ & $t \leqslant 20$ & $t \leqslant 35$ & $t \leqslant 60$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 3 & 10 & & & 80 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram to show this information.
\newline

 Find an estimate for the 80th percentile.
\newline

 Find an estimate for the percentage of people who took longer than 45 minutes to travel to work.
\newline
\\
Show all your working.
\newline

content.content:
Write down the class interval containing the median time.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the mean time.
\newline

One of these 80 people is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this person took longer than 10 minutes to travel to work.
\newline
\\
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

Two people are chosen at random from those taking 20 minutes or less to travel to work.
\newline

Calculate the probability that one of these people took 5 minutes or less and the other took more than 5 minutes.
\newline

Use the frequency table to complete the cumulative frequency table.
\newline

On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram to show this information.
\newline

Find an estimate for the 80th percentile.
\newline

Find an estimate for the percentage of people who took longer than 45 minutes to travel to work.
\newline
\\
Show all your working.
\newline

stem.content:
The time, $t$ minutes, taken by each of 80 people to travel to work is recorded.
\newline
\\
The table shows information about these times.
\newline

qtype: simplifying expressions, expanding and simplifying, combining algebraic fractions, solving linear equations, changing the subject of a formula
tex:
(a) Simplify.
\newline
\[a - 2b - 3a + 7b\]

(b) Expand and simplify.
\newline
\[4(x - 5) - (3 - 2x)\]

(c) Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline
\[\frac{3}{x - 5} - \frac{7}{2x}\]

(d) Solve.
\newline
\[\frac{13 - 4x}{3} = 6 - x\]

(e) Make $x$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
\[y = \frac{5(p - 2x)}{x}\]

content.content:
Simplify.
\newline
\[a - 2b - 3a + 7b\]

Expand and simplify.
\newline
\[4(x - 5) - (3 - 2x)\]

Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline
\[\frac{3}{x - 5} - \frac{7}{2x}\]

Solve.
\newline
\[\frac{13 - 4x}{3} = 6 - x\]

Make $x$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
\[y = \frac{5(p - 2x)}{x}\]

qtype: sketching cubic graphs, expanding algebraic expressions, finding turning points using differentiation, analyzing number of solutions from graph
tex:
(a) Sketch the graph of $y = (x + 1)(3 - x)(3 + x)$, indicating the coordinates of the points where the graph crosses the $x$-axis and the $y$-axis.
\newline

(b) (i)  Show that $y = (x + 1)(3 - x)(3 + x)$ can be written as $y = 9 + 9x - x^2 - x^3$.
\newline

 Calculate the $x$-values of the turning points of $y = 9 + 9x - x^2 - x^3$.
\newline
\\
Show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

{...}]

 The equation $9 + 9x - x^2 - x^3 = k$ has one solution only when $k < a$ and when $k > b$, where $a$ and $b$ are integers.
\newline

Find the maximum value of $a$ and the minimum value of $b$.
\newline

\newline
